alignments.preprint_claim_text: TDGIA significantly outperforms various attack baselines in attacking multiple defense GNN models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: TDGIA significantly outperforms various attack baselines in attacking multiple defense GNN models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is consistent across preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance drop on target GNNs caused by TDGIA is more than double the damage from the best attack solution among hundreds of submissions on KDD-CUP 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance drop on target GNNs from TDGIA is more than double the damage caused by the best attack solution among hundreds of submissions on KDD-CUP 2020. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same quantitative comparison retained; wording slightly reordered but meaning identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random forests are effective tools to predict the output of cosmological simulations without running the full process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Random forests are effective tools to predict the output of cosmological simulations without running the full process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim content and certainty are preserved; only ordering of claims differs in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The properties of the matter density field in the initial conditions have a decisive impact on the features of the structures formed today. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The properties of the matter density field in the initial conditions have a decisive impact on the features of the structures formed today. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion maintained between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The techniques developed in this paper might be used in the future to decrease the computational time and to explore more efficiently the effect of different dark matter/dark energy candidates on the formation of cosmological structures. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The techniques used in this study might be employed in the future to decrease computational time and explore the effects of different dark matter/dark energy candidates on the formation of cosmological structures. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Future-use contribution is preserved with minor rewording; scope and confidence unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prediction performance of a wide range of GNN models is highly correlated with the node level assortativity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prediction performance of a wide range of GNN models is highly correlated with the node level assortativity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conventional global measurement of assortativity may not be a representative statistic in quantifying mixing patterns in real world networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conventional global measurement of assortativity may not be a representative statistic in quantifying diverse mixing patterns in real world networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds 'diverse' to qualify mixing patterns, a small clarification that does not materially broaden or narrow the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transforming the input graph into a computation graph enhances the level of assortativity and preserves rich information from the original graph. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transforming the input graph into a computation graph enhances the level of assortativity and preserves rich information from the original graph. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The content is the same but the publication frames this as a contribution (method) rather than a direct result, a reframing/clarification of the claim's role.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptively choosing between structure and proximity in GNNs leads to improved performance under diverse mixing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptively choosing between structure and proximity in GNNs leads to improved performance under diverse mixing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is retained verbatim (aside from identical wording) with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNNs outperform ANNs in terms of overall accuracy by over 15% when the data is distributed across a large number of clients in the federation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) outperform Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) in terms of overall accuracy by over 15 when the data is distributed across a large number of clients in the federation. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNNs provide up to 5.3x energy efficiency compared to traditional ANNs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNNs provide up to 4.3 times energy efficiency compared to ANNs. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed federated learning framework enables decentralized and privacy-preserving training of SNNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed federated learning method enables training decentralized and privacy-preserving SNNs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The sensitivity of the proposed federated SNN framework to data distribution among clients, stragglers, and gradient noise is analyzed. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sensitivity of the proposed federated SNN framework to data distribution among clients, stragglers, and gradient noise has been analyzed. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Locating meteorites can be automated using machine learning and an autonomous drone. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system was tested in the suspected strewn field of a recent meteorite fall near Walker Lake, Nevada. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The system was tested in the suspected strewn field of a recent meteorite fall near Walker Lake, Nevada. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The test/location result is reported identically in the publication with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Active Learning (AL) can achieve high accuracy in Network Traffic Classification (NTC) with a small amount of data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Active Learning (AL) can achieve high accuracy in Network Traffic Classification (NTC) with a small amount of data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine Learning (ML) algorithms for NTC face challenges due to the shortage of labeled traffic data in real-world applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine Learning (ML) algorithms for NTC face challenges due to the shortage of labeled traffic data in real-world applications. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same challenge/observation with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides an overview of the fundamental challenges in NTC and surveys existing ML-based NTC methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides an overview of NTC and its fundamental challenges, along with a survey of ML-based NTC methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning is a promising solution for reversible steganography. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the general claim that 'deep learning is a promising solution'; it focuses instead on a modular paradigm and specific analyses, so this high-level optimistic claim was removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modular paradigm incorporating neural networks into traditional pipelines can stably guarantee reversibility with mathematical explainability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modular paradigm that incorporates neural networks into traditional pipelines can stably guarantee reversibility with mathematical explainability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core conclusion verbatim, with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different initialisation strategies for input images may affect the learning process of neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different initialisation strategies for input images affect the learning process of neural networks. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same effect more directly (removing 'may'), so the claim is slightly clarified while maintaining scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different training strategies for dual-layer prediction respond to the problem of distributional shift. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different training configurations impact the predictive accuracy of neural networks in the analytics module of reversible steganography. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's claim about addressing distributional shift into a more general statement about training configuration impacts on accuracy, narrowing the specific problem addressed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study compares steganographic performance of various model architectures with different loss functions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Various model architectures with different loss functions exhibit differing steganographic performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same comparative result regarding architectures and loss functions affecting steganographic performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed data-driven framework results in a significant reduction in error of the predicted trajectories compared to comparable strategies in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-driven framework results in a significant reduction in error of the predicted trajectories compared to comparable strategies in the literature. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result claim verbatim with equivalent certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model learns from past observed trajectories and leverages traversability information to produce a set of future trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model learns from past observed trajectories and leverages traversability information to produce a set of future trajectories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented identically in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework can be used with a small, un-annotated dataset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework can be used with a small, unannotated dataset. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same conclusion (minor punctuation difference only), so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The sentiment analysis on collected tweets related to Covid-19 indicates a trend in public sentiment during the pandemic.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study calculated sentiment ratings on tweets based on their classification.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ADVERT generates adaptive visual aids in real-time to prevent users from inadvertence and reduce their susceptibility to phishing attacks.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The meta-adaptation can further improve the accuracy to 91.5% (resp. 93.7%) in less than 3 (resp. 50) tuning stages.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The AutoScore-Survival-derived scoring system was more parsimonious than survival models built using traditional variable selection methods.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoScore-Survival achieved a comparable integrated area under the curve of 0.782.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The integer-valued time-to-event scores generated by AutoScore-Survival are easier to compute and interpret, making them favorable in clinical applications.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical foundations to detect and quantify conceptual errors in data scalings are presented in this work.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An experimental investigation of the proposed approach was conducted on eleven data sets treated with a variant of non-negative matrix factorization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An experimental investigation of the proposed approach was conducted on eleven data sets treated with a variant of non-negative matrix factorization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The conceptual continuity of the reduction mapping is a previously unconsidered criterion in dimension reduction procedures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The conceptual continuity of the reduction mapping is a previously unconsidered criterion in dimension reduction procedures.
alignments.rationale: Publication elevates this point to a contribution with higher stated certainty (medium -> high), amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end differentiable approach enables the extraction of logic explanations from neural networks using the formalism of First-Order Logic.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end differentiable approach enables the extraction of logic explanations from neural networks using the formalism of First-Order Logic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This entropy-based criterion enables the distillation of concise logic explanations in safety-critical domains from clinical data to computer vision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method provides concise logic explanations in safety-critical domains from clinical data to computer vision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art white-box models in terms of classification accuracy and matches black box performances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art white-box models in terms of classification accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The horizontal collaboration among resource-constrained AIoT node devices increases the attack surface for CNN-based applications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed FeSHI attack exploits the statistical distribution of layer-by-layer feature maps in CNN architectures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FeSHI attack exploits the statistical distribution of layer-by-layer feature maps of CNNs to design stealthy hardware Trojans.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two triggers for stealthy hardware Trojans with a very low probability of triggering are designed based on the Gaussian distribution of feature maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attack triggers designed in FeSHI vary randomly, making it extremely difficult to detect.
alignments.rationale: The publication removes the specific "two triggers / very low probability" formulation and instead emphasizes randomness and detectability, changing the technical specifics while asserting the claim with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three different novel, stealthy, and effective trigger designs for hardware Trojans are proposed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attack triggers designed in FeSHI vary randomly, making it extremely difficult to detect.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the stealth/detectability aspect of the trigger designs rather than enumerating "three" novel triggers, clarifying the practical implication and expressing higher confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a new model of event data that captures its natural spatio-temporal structure.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a new model of event data that captures its natural spatio-temporal structure.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We develop a maximum likelihood approach to registering events that are not yet aligned.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We develop a maximum likelihood approach to registering events that are not yet aligned.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this methodological contribution without alteration.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We show new state of the art accuracy for rotational velocity estimation on the DAVIS 240C dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show new state of the art accuracy for rotational velocity estimation on the DAVIS 240C dataset.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is faster and has lower computational complexity than several competing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method is faster and has lower computational complexity than several competing methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the runtime and complexity claims exactly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A sign constraint on the weights is a necessary condition for system recovery.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A sign constraint on the weights is a necessary condition for system recovery.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the necessary-condition result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study establishes an identifiability guarantee for the recovery of the underlying biological circuit.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study establishes an identifiability guarantee for the recovery of the underlying biological circuit.
alignments.rationale: The identifiability guarantee is preserved unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates practical potential through a case study on retinal ganglion cell circuits using data collected from mouse retina.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach demonstrates practical potential in recovering models of retinal ganglion cell circuits using data collected from mouse retina.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the practical demonstration by specifying 'recovering models' of retinal ganglion cell circuits, a modest wording change without altering scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel method for constructing symmetry-preserving attention networks significantly outperforms current methods in reconstructing particle assignments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPANet demonstrates improved efficiency in handling particle physics data.
alignments.rationale: The publication tones down the strong "significantly outperforms" performance claim to a more general statement of improved efficiency, reducing the claim's strength and specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new approach improves reconstruction efficiency between 19% - 35% on typical benchmark problems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPANet demonstrates improved efficiency in handling particle physics data.
alignments.rationale: The published paper keeps the efficiency improvement claim but omits the specific numeric range, weakening and narrowing the quantified scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method decreases inference time by two to five orders of magnitude on the most complex events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPANet demonstrates improved efficiency in handling particle physics data.
alignments.rationale: A strong, specific speedup claim is reduced to a general efficiency improvement in the publication, representing a substantive revision and tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current strategies for tackling particle assignment challenges do not scale to complex configurations.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state this limitation of current strategies, so the preprint's explicit claim about lack of scalability appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a current term NISQ algorithm for solving semidefinite programs (SDPs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a current term NISQ algorithm for solving semidefinite programs (SDPs).
alignments.rationale: The core contribution of a NISQ SDP algorithm is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classical optimization program of our NISQ eigensolver is convex, can be solved in polynomial time, and every local minimum is a global minimum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical optimization program of our NISQ eigensolver is convex and can be solved in polynomial time.
alignments.rationale: The publication omits the explicit statement that every local minimum is global, reducing the claim's explicitness while keeping convexity and polynomial-time solvability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We find numeric evidence that NISQ SDP can improve the estimation of ground state energies in a scalable manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NISQ SDP can improve the estimation of ground-state energies in a scalable manner.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the potential of our approach by finding the largest eigenvalue of up to 21000 dimensional matrices.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work extends the application of NISQ computers onto one of the most successful algorithmic frameworks of the past few decades.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our work extends the application of NISQ computers onto one of the most successful algorithmic frameworks of the past few decades.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms can generalize low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) signals to high SNR ones but not vice versa. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning algorithms can generalize low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) signals to high SNR ones but not vice versa. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is not beneficial to provide high SNR signals during training for deep learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: It is not beneficial to provide high SNR signals during training for deep learning algorithms. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fastest convergence of deep learning networks is achieved when low SNR samples are provided early on during training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The fastest convergence of deep learning networks is achieved when low SNR samples are provided early on during training. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The networks are sometimes unable to recover any signals when a false alarm probability <10-3 is required. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying a modification called unbounded Softmax replacement (USR) after training allows the machine learning search to retain ≥91.5% of the sensitivity of the matched-filter search down to a false-alarm rate of 1 per month. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: LSTM outperformed PLSTM on six of seven data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: It is possible to learn to localize actions in untrimmed videos with only one/few trimmed examples of the target action available at test time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed network estimates Temporal Similarity Matrices (TSMs) to model fine-grained similarity patterns between pairs of videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Temporal Class Activation Maps (TCAMs) generated by the network serve as temporal attention mechanisms to extract video-level representations of untrimmed videos. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves performance comparable or better to state-of-the-art fully-supervised, few-shot learning methods on the THUMOS14 and ActivityNet1.2 datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Artificial Neural Network took more time in training and validating the model compared to other models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial Neural Network took more time in training and validating the model compared to other models.
alignments.rationale: No substantive change between preprint and publication for this timing/performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Support Vector Machine performed better than the rest of the models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Support Vector Machine performed better than the rest of the models.
alignments.rationale: The performance ranking claim is identical in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stochastic Gradient Descent performed better than Support Vector Machine with an increase in the size of the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Stochastic Gradient Descent performed better than Support Vector Machine with an increase in the size of the dataset.
alignments.rationale: This relative-performance claim is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mercury constitutive model plays a central role in improving the lifetime prediction and future target designs of the mercury targets at the Spallation Neutron Source (SNS).
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate this broad conclusion about the constitutive model's central role; the claim is omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The newly calibrated simulations achieve 7% average improvement on the signal prediction accuracy and 8% reduction in mean absolute error compared to previously reported reference parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The newly calibrated simulations achieve 7% average improvement on the prediction accuracy compared to previously reported reference parameters.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the numerical results into separate claims (7% accuracy improvement and 8% MAE reduction). The 7% component maps directly to this preprint claim, so the result is clarified into more granular statements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed calibrated simulations can significantly aid in fatigue analysis to estimate the mercury target lifetime and integrity, reducing abrupt target failure and saving costs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The calibrated simulations can aid in fatigue analysis to estimate the mercury target lifetime, reducing abrupt failure and saving costs.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the fatigue-analysis / lifetime-estimation utility claim with essentially the same wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a deficiency in the current constitutive model based on the equation of state in capturing the full physics of the spallation reaction.
alignments.rationale: The identified limitation in the constitutive model present in the preprint is not included among the publication's stated claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A more advanced two-phase flow model is needed to capture bubble dynamics and mercury cavitation.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for a two-phase flow model and explicit mention of cavitation/bubble dynamics does not appear in the publication's listed claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic control is recognized as an instance of Follow-The-Leader (FTL).
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic control is recognized as an instance of Follow‐The‐Leader (FTL).
alignments.rationale: The conceptual identification of synthetic control with FTL is carried over verbatim to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic control predictions of counterfactual outcomes for the treated unit perform almost as well as an oracle weighted average of control units' outcomes, even when outcomes are chosen by an adversary.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic control predictions of counterfactual outcomes for the treated unit perform almost as well as an oracle weighted average of control units' outcomes, even when outcomes are chosen by an adversary.
alignments.rationale: This theoretical performance guarantee is preserved exactly in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic control on differenced data performs almost as well as oracle weighted difference-in-differences.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic control on differenced data performs almost as well as oracle weighted difference‐in‐differences.
alignments.rationale: The claim is present in the publication but reported with slightly lower stated certainty (publication labels it medium rather than high), so confidence is modestly tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The observation that synthetic control performs well supports the use of synthetic control estimators in comparative case studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The observation that synthetic control performs well supports the use of synthetic control estimators in comparative case studies.
alignments.rationale: The methodological recommendation based on the results is carried forward unchanged in sense and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Outsourced printed circuit board (PCB) fabrication necessitates increased hardware assurance capabilities.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not include this broad conclusion about necessity; instead the publication lists prior AOI assurance techniques, so the preprint's high-level necessity claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a strong, rapid trend toward machine learning (ML) solutions in automated optical inspection (AOI) techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a strong, rapid trend toward ML solutions in AOI techniques.
alignments.rationale: The trend observation is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The FICS PCB Image Collection (FPIC) dataset is contributed to address the lack of labeled ground truth data in the publicly available PCB data space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The FPIC dataset is contributed to address the lack of labeled ground truth data in the publicly available PCB data space.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New hardware security methodologies are outlined, enabled by the FPIC dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: New hardware security methodologies are outlined, enabled by the FPIC dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution of new security methodologies enabled by the dataset with the same scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Manuscript symbols can be stored, recognized, and retrieved from an entropic digital memory that is associative and distributed. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The memory recognition operation obeys an entropy trade-off between precision and recall. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The memory recognition operation obeys an entropy trade-off between precision and recall. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same trade-off claim with no substantive change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The entropy level impacts the quality of the objects recovered through the memory retrieval operation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The entropy level impacts the quality of the objects recovered through the memory retrieval operation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in the publication; content and confidence are preserved.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The experiments reported in this paper add evidence on the potential of this framework for developing practical applications and computational models of natural memory. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed continuous model, MTGODE, addresses the limitations of existing methods by using dynamic graphs with time-evolving node features and unknown graph structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MTGODE abstracts multivariate time series into dynamic graphs with time-evolving node features and unknown graph structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MTGODE allows deeper graph propagation and fine-grained temporal information aggregation to characterize stable and precise latent spatial-temporal dynamics. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the mechanism as Graph neural ODEs rather than stating the specific downstream effects (deeper propagation, fine-grained aggregation); this is a rephrasing/clarification of the technical approach rather than an outright contradiction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the superiorities of MTGODE on five time series benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the superiorities of MTGODE on five time series benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority on the same five benchmarks is retained in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Computing-in-Memory architectures based on non-volatile emerging memories have demonstrated great potential for deep neural network acceleration due to their high energy efficiency. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: It is only necessary to select a small portion of the weights for write-verify to maintain the DNN accuracy, thus achieving significant speedup. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SWIM technique can achieve up to 10x programming speedup compared with conventional full-blown write-verify while attaining a comparable accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the applicability advantage with clearer context (explicitly comparing to prior APR techniques) but preserves the substantive claim and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There isn't any overview on the field of Neural Program Repair (NPR) yet.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper undertakes a literature review of the latest NPR systems to help readers understand advancements in this emerging field.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's assertion that no overview exists is contradicted by the publication, which presents such a review; this revises the original negative claim and reduces the original certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper conducts a literature review on the latest studies in Neural Program Repair (NPR).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper undertakes a literature review of the latest NPR systems to help readers understand advancements in this emerging field.
alignments.rationale: This contribution is present in both versions with essentially the same meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The NPR procedure is decomposed into a series of modules with various design choices explicated for each module.
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not describe a modular decomposition or design-choice exposition, so this preprint claim does not appear in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several challenges in Neural Program Repair (NPR) are identified and the effect of existing solutions is discussed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The authors highlight a number of challenges and summarize corresponding solutions adopted by existing NPR systems.
alignments.rationale: The identification of challenges and summary of solutions is present in both versions with consistent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed contrastive-mixup strategy improves robustness and generalization in speaker verification tasks. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed contrastive-mixup augmentation strategy learns distinguishing representations based on a distance metric. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution by describing the mechanism (learning distinguishing representations via a distance metric) rather than the broader claim about robustness/generalization, so the claim is clarified rather than removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying contrastive-mixup reduces the error rate by 16% relative to existing baselines. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying contrastive-mixup outperforms the existing baseline, reducing error rate by 16% relatively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical result and comparison are the same in both versions, so the claim is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Contrastive-mixup is particularly effective when the number of training utterances per speaker is limited. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Contrastive-mixup demonstrates improved generalization given limited training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both statements convey the same conclusion about improved performance under limited training data, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed augmentation of existing iterative phase retrieval algorithms with a neural network improves convergence rates. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An augmentation of existing iterative phase retrieval algorithms with a neural network is proposed for refining the result of each iteration. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the proposed augmentation and its per-iteration refinement role (clarifying mechanism) rather than only stating the improvement in convergence rates.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enhances both iteration count and algorithm runtime in ptychography. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach delivers improved convergence rates in terms of both iteration count and algorithm runtime. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claims (iteration count and runtime) for ptychography, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network is adapted and extended from a recently proposed architecture in the speech processing field. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network architecture is adapted and extended from a recently proposed architecture in the speech processing field. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same origin/adaptation of the neural network architecture, so this contribution is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SapientML can efficiently generate a high-quality pipeline for a predictive task on a new dataset by learning from a corpus of existing datasets and their human-written pipelines. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SapientML employs a novel divide-and-conquer strategy realized as a three-stage program synthesis approach. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the approach in terms of a divide-and-conquer three-stage program synthesis; this clarifies the implementation of the preprint's higher-level claim about generating pipelines from previous examples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SapientML employs a novel divide-and-conquer strategy realized as a three-stage program synthesis approach to combat the search space explosion of AutoML. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SapientML employs a novel divide-and-conquer strategy realized as a three-stage program synthesis approach. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This contribution is stated essentially identically in the publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our evaluation shows that SapientML produces the best or comparable accuracy on 27 of the benchmarks while the second best tool fails to even produce a pipeline on 9 of the instances. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SapientML produces the best or comparable accuracy on 27 of the benchmarks evaluated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the primary evaluation result (27 benchmarks) explicitly; the preprint combined this with an additional failure-mode statement (covered separately in the publication).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: t-METASET is an active-learning-based data acquisition framework that guides diverse and task-aware data generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: t-METASET is an active learning-based data acquisition framework that guides diverse and task-aware data generation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework addresses the challenge of data-driven design of metamaterials when a large shape-only library has been prepared without evaluated properties. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework addresses the challenge of designing metamaterials when a large shape-only library has been prepared without evaluated properties. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same problem addressed by the framework; the change in label (limitation vs contribution) does not alter the claim content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficacy of t-METASET is validated in three deployment cases, including general use, task-specific use, and tailorable use. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficacy of t-METASET is validated in three deployment cases, including general use, task-specific use, and tailorable use. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The validation in three deployment cases is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two large-scale mechanical metamaterial datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two large-scale mechanical metamaterial datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This empirical result is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: t-METASET could enhance future advancements in data-driven design applicable to general image-based design representations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The forward-looking generalization claim about future advancements is not present in the publication claims and thus appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed pre-training scheme and new loss function improve the critical success index (CSI) of nowcasting of heavy rainfall by up to 95.7% and 43.6%, respectively, at a 5-hr lead time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our pre-training scheme improves the critical success index (CSI) of nowcasting of heavy rainfall (at least 10 mm/hr) by up to 95.7% at a 5-hr lead time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the combined preprint claim into distinct claims and specifies the heavy-rainfall threshold for the pre-training result; this clarifies and slightly narrows the original combined statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach reduces the precipitation estimation error by up to 10.7% for light rainfall compared to the conventional approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach reduces the precipitation estimation error by up to 10.7% for light rainfall (between 1 and 10 mm/hr) compared to the conventional approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the numeric improvement and adds a specific rainfall-range definition, which clarifies but does not change the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is adapted to address both precipitation nowcasting and precipitation estimation from radar images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a pre-training scheme and a new loss function for improving deep-learning-based nowcasting. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication focuses the contribution on nowcasting (pre-training and loss) and does not explicitly claim the broader adaptation to both nowcasting and estimation, so the original claim is narrowed and its confidence appears tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The pre-training scheme does not require ground-truth precipitation for predicting radar images in the near future. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The specific assertion that pre-training does not require ground-truth precipitation is not present among the publication's summarized claims, so it appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We established a highly effective and efficient pipeline to extract cultivated land from high-resolution remote sensing images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We established a highly effective and efficient pipeline to solve the problem of cultivated land extraction from high-resolution remote sensing images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent strength and scope; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We developed a set of effective methods for instance segmentation that are applicable to remote sensing images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We explored several instance segmentation algorithms that work well on natural images and developed effective methods applicable to remote sensing images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published version adds context about exploring algorithms from natural images and reduces stated certainty, clarifying the approach while softening confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We achieved the first place among 486 teams in the iFLYTEK challenge 2021 for cultivated land extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieved first place among 486 teams in the iFLYTEK challenge 2021 for cultivated land extraction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is reported identically in the publication with no material change in wording or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning has delivered little value to the speech science community due to its inability to meet controllability demands. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning has delivered little value to the speech science community despite revolutionising synthetic speech quality. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication softens and reframes the original causal explanation (removes explicit 'inability to meet controllability demands') and reduces stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Legacy signal-processing methods are currently used to control different speech properties in manipulated speech stimuli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Control of different speech properties in manipulated stimuli is currently achieved using legacy signal-processing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same factual claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Audible artefacts negatively impact the methodological validity of results in speech perception studies. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Audible artefacts negatively impact the methodological validity of results in speech perception studies. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced system can manipulate speech properties through learning rather than design, achieving realistic output. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduced system can manipulate speech properties through learning rather than design. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the approach can produce speech stimuli with accurate control and high perceptual quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the approach can produce speech stimuli with accurate control and high perceptual quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Experimental results are reported equivalently in both versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed MDT-driven Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithm outperforms baseline approaches like DQN and best-first search in terms of long-term reward and sample efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed MDT-driven Deep Reinforcement Learning algorithm outperforms baseline approaches like DQN and best-first search in terms of long-term reward and sample efficiency. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tweaks introduced to the classical DQN formulation improve the agent's sample efficiency, stability, and performance. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tweaks introduced to the classical DQN formulation improve the agent’s sample efficiency, stability, and performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: MDT-driven approaches constitute a valuable tool for autonomous coverage and capacity optimization of mobile radio networks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this concluding assessment without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper is the first to describe the 'speciesist bias' and investigate it in several different AI systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper is the first to describe the ‘speciesist bias’ in AI systems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Speciesist biases are learned and solidified by AI applications when they are trained on datasets in which speciesist patterns prevail. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evidence for speciesist biases is found in image recognition, word embedding, and language models. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI technologies currently play a significant role in perpetuating and normalizing violence against animals. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI technologies currently play a significant role in perpetuating and normalizing violence against animals. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AI fairness frameworks must widen their scope to include mitigation measures for speciesist biases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AI fairness frameworks must widen their scope to include mitigation measures for speciesist biases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The influence of AI systems can either increase or reduce the violence inflicted on animals, especially on farmed animals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding claim about dual-directional influence on animal violence appears in the publication; this specific conditional claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural networks can be effectively distilled from an ensemble of regression trees, making them a competitor to tree-based ensembles on the ranking task. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks can effectively be distilled from an ensemble of regression trees, making them a natural competitor for ranking tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Ensembles of regression trees outperform neural models in terms of efficiency and effectiveness, especially when scoring on CPU. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach combines Distillation, Pruning, and Fast Matrix multiplication to speed up neural scoring time. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks produced with the proposed approach provide up to 4x scoring time speed-up without affecting ranking quality. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PTMDA improves the performance on the real target domain by exploiting additional structured source information through training on pseudo target domains. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: PTMDA outperforms state-of-the-art methods in most experimental settings for MDA tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Replacing the traditional batch normalization layer with a matching normalization layer in DNNs improves their transferability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Replacing the traditional batch normalization layer with a matching normalization layer in DNNs improves their transferability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Build2Vec method has better scalability potential than conventional zoning and generic grid methods. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The applicability of the POD-Galerkin method to non linear high-dimensional dynamical systems such as the Navier–Stokes equations has been shown to be limited, producing inaccurate and sometimes unstable models. (certainty=high, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed CD-ROM approach is based on an interpretable continuous memory formulation, derived from simple hypotheses on the behavior of partially observed dynamical systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework achieves an overall detection accuracy of 96.88% for brain tumor detection. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework generates a large synthetic MRI images dataset from a small class-unbalanced collected dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework generates a large synthetic MRI images dataset from a small class-unbalanced collected dataset. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reward shaping strategy successfully identified samples that need re-acquisition due to defected imaging processes. (certainty=high, type=result)
